name: chat-short-failover
description: Scenario B / I3 failover demonstration workload: steady 0.08 rps streaming traffic for 360 s with a constant 200-token output cap (long-ish streams so the mid-run llama-server SIGKILL reliably catches accepted streams in flight). run.sh kills llama-server once a slot is processing (~T+110-130 s) and restarts it at ~T+240 s; the gateway runs the IG-T005 static-priority Failover pair (llamacpp primary, mock fallback). Expected client-visible behavior per infergate@74f2372: streams accepted before the kill end with exactly one typed upstream_error SSE event (never failed over -- tokens already delivered must not be duplicated); every request dispatched while llama-server is down is served by the mock (one failed dial each, then fallback, zero client-visible errors); after the restart, per-request primary-first selection returns traffic to llama-server automatically.
